    Sign in

    Compare Surface computers

    Find the best computer for you by comparing specs and functionality. Every Surface computer includes a touchscreen with 3:2 aspect ratio for fast, intuitive navigation. No matter which Surface you're comparing, stay connected anywhere with extra-long battery life2, and the protection, privacy, and peace of mind from Windows 11.

    Still wondering which Surface is right for you?

    Answer a few short questions about what’s important to you, and we’ll recommend the perfect Surface computer based on your specific needs.
    Help me choose

    Protect your Surface

    Keep your new Surface computer up and running with accidental damage protection18 from drops, spills, and cracked screens. With Microsoft Complete, if something goes wrong, you’re covered – simple as that.
    Learn about Surface Protection plans

    Frequently asked questions

    |

    • Choosing the right computer depends on how—and where—you plan to use it. Here are a few key factors to consider:

      • Your primary use case: Will you mostly be working, creating, streaming, or multitasking? Your activities help determine the processing power, storage, and memory you'll need.
      • Where you’ll use it: If you’re on the go, look for lightweight options like Surface Laptops.6
      • Device type that fits your style: Consider whether you want a classic laptop, a 2-in-1 for flexibility, or an all-in-one desktop for a larger workspace.
      • AI-ready features: Look for a computer with next-level AI experiences, like Copilot+ PCs.**

    • New Surface PCs running Windows 11 come with Copilot on Windows pre-installed.

    • Yes. New Surface computers come with a 1-year limited warranty in the US, but the length varies by market.

    • Our touchscreen laptops, 2-in-1s, and all-in-one PCs are designed to help you work, play, and create in your own unique way.

    • The key difference between Surface Laptop and Surface Pro is form factor: Surface Pro is a 2-in-1 device that functions as both a tablet and laptop, while Surface Laptop is a traditional laptop.

      • Surface Pro: A lightweight,6,17 versatile 2-in-1 device that works as both a tablet and laptop. Pair it with accessories including Surface Slim Pen and a detachable keyboard.
      • Surface Laptop: A traditional laptop with a built-in keyboard, slim design,17 and long battery life2—great if you want a classic laptop experience with touchscreen functionality.


      Learn more about the differences between the Surface Pro and Surface Laptop.

    • The difference between a laptop, tablet, and 2-in-1 PC comes down to how each device is built and how you plan to use it.

      • Laptop: A portable computer with a built-in keyboard, generally designed for productivity and everyday work.
      • Tablet: A lightweight touchscreen device built for portability, entertainment, and simple tasks.
      • 2-in-1 PC: A versatile device that can function as both a laptop and tablet—often featuring a detachable or foldable keyboard—so you can switch between keyboard typing and touch-only modes as needed.

    • Yes. Microsoft offers a full lineup of Surface PCs designed for business, including models like Surface Pro 10 for Business or Surface Laptop 6 for Business. These business devices include:

      • Windows 11 Pro
      • Chip-to-cloud security features
      • Long battery life2
      • Copilot+ PC AI experiences**
      • Management tools such as Windows Autopilot32


      Learn more about Surface for Business Laptops and Computers.

    • When you compare business laptops vs. consumer laptops in the Surface lineup, the biggest differences usually come down to software, level of security, and how the devices are meant to be used.31

      • Consumer laptops: Surface Pro or Surface Laptop consumer laptops come with Windows 11 and security features including a Microsoft Pluton34 security chip that helps protect credentials, user identities, encryption keys, and personal data. The Windows Hello35,36 uses face authentication with Enhanced Sign-in security, all for the purpose of everyday computing.
      • Business laptops: Surface Pro for Business or Surface Laptop for Business—comes with Windows 11 Pro, additional chip-to-cloud security including Bitlocker,37 remote firmware management,33 and other features designed for businesses.
    • Protect your Surface
    • Surface for your business
    • Accessories
    • Surface Support
    • Register your Surface
    • Power cord recall
    Features and app availability may vary by region.
    • [*] Optimized for touch interaction only. Pen input and compatibility with Surface Pen, Surface Slim Pen, or similar accessories is not supported.
    • [**] Copilot+ PC experiences vary by device and market and may require updates continuing to roll out; timing varies.
    • [1] System software and updates use significant storage space. Available storage is subject to change based on system software updates and apps usage. 1 GB = 1 billion bytes. 1 TB = 1,000 GB. See Surface.com/Storage for more details.
    • [2] Battery life varies significantly based on device configuration, usage, network and feature configuration, signal strength, settings and other factors. See Surface Battery Performance for details.

      Surface Pro 13" Wi-Fi only models-connected using Wi-Fi 
      Local video playback: Based on local video playback test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in April 2024 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Pro Snapdragon® X Plus C10 256GB and 512GB, 16GB RAM (LCD), Wi-Fi only devices and Surface Pro Snapdragon® X Elite C12 1TB 32GB RAM (OLED), Wi-Fi only devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge during video playback of a .mov file through the Windows Media Player application in 1080p at 24 FPS. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Web browsing: Based on a web browsing test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in April 2024 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Pro Snapdragon® X Plus C10 256GB and 512GB, 16GB RAM (LCD), Wi-Fi only devices and Surface Pro Snapdragon® X Elite C12 1TB 32GB RAM (OLED), Wi-Fi only devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge while accessing eight popular websites over multiple open tabs through the browser. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Surface Pro 13" Wi-Fi+5G models-connected using cellular
      Web browsing: Based on a web browsing test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in August 2024 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Pro Snapdragon® X Plus C10 256GB, 16GB RAM (LCD) and Surface Pro Snapdragon® X Elite C12 512GB, 16GB RAM (OLED) devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge while accessing eight popular websites over multiple open tabs through the browser. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness disabled. Cellular enabled and device connected to a cellular network. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Surface Pro Wi-Fi only models-connected using Wi-Fi
      Local video playback: Based on local video playback test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in March 2025 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Pro 12-inch, Copilot+ PC, Snapdragon® X Plus (8 Core) 256GB, 16GB RAM 1st Edition devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge during video playback of a .mov file through the Windows Media Player application in 1080p at 24 FPS. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Web browsing: Based on a web browsing test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in March 2025 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Pro 12-inch, Copilot+ PC, Snapdragon® X Plus (8 Core) 256GB, 16GB RAM 1st Edition devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge while accessing 12 popular websites over multiple open tabs through the browser. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Surface Laptop
      Local video playback: Based on local video playback test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in March 2025 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Laptop 13-inch, Copilot+ PC, Snapdragon® X Plus (8 Core) 256GB, 16GB RAM 1st Edition devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge during video playback of a .mov file through the Windows Media Player application in 1080p at 24 FPS. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Web browsing: Based on a web browsing test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in March 2025 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Laptop 13-inch, Copilot+ PC, Snapdragon® X Plus (8 Core) 256GB, 16GB RAM 1st Edition devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge while accessing twelve popular websites over multiple open tabs through the browser. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Surface Laptop 13.8"
      Based on a local video playback test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in January 2025 for Wi-Fi only models and in July 2025 for Wi-fi+5G models using preproduction software and Surface Laptop for Business, 13.8-inch Intel Core Ultra 5 256GB, 16GB RAM devices and Surface Laptop for Business, 15-inch Intel Core Ultra 7 256GB, 16GB RAM devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge while accessing eight popular websites over multiple open tabs through the browser. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network for Wi-Fi only models and cellular was connected to a cellular network for Wi-Fi and 5G models. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Surface Laptop 15"
      Local video playback: Based on local video playback test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in April 2024 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Laptop Snapdragon® X Elite C12 512GB, 16GB RAM devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge during video playback of a .mov file through the Windows Media Player application in 1080p at 24 FPS. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Tested with Windows 11. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Web browsing: Based on web browsing test. Testing conducted by Microsoft in April 2024 using preproduction software and preproduction Surface Laptop Snapdragon® X Elite C12 512GB, 16GB RAM devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge while accessing eight popular websites over multiple open tabs through the browser. All settings were default except screen brightness set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Tested with Windows 11. Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Surface Laptop Studio 2:
      Up to 19.0 hours of battery life based on typical Surface device usage on Surface Laptop Studio 2 with Intel® Core™ i7-13700H Processor, 512GB, 16GB RAM - Testing conducted by Microsoft in July-August 2023 using preproduction Windows 11 software and preproduction Surface Laptop Studio 2 Intel® Core™ i7-13700H Processor, 512GB, 16GB RAM devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge with a mixture of active use and modern standby. The active use portion consists of (1) a web browsing test accessing eight popular websites over multiple open tabs, (2) a productivity test utilizing Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel, OneNote and Outlook, and (3) a portion of time with the device in use with idle applications. All settings were default except screen brightness was set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness, Adaptive Color, and Smart Charging disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Tested with Windows 11 Version 10.0.22621 (22H2). Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Up to 18.0 hours of battery life based on typical Surface device usage on Surface Laptop Studio 2 with Intel® Core™ i7-13700H Processor, NVIDIA® GeForce™ RTX 4050 Laptop GPU, 512GB, 16GB RAM. Testing conducted by Microsoft in July-August 2023 using preproduction Windows 11 software and preproduction Surface Laptop Studio 2 Intel® Core™ i7-13700H Processor, NVIDIA® GeForce™ RTX 4050 Laptop GPU, 512GB, 16GB RAM devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge with a mixture of active use and modern standby. The active use portion consists of (1) a web browsing test accessing eight popular websites over multiple open tabs, (2) a productivity test utilizing Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel, OneNote and Outlook, and (3) a portion of time with the device in use with idle applications. All settings were default except screen brightness was set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness, Adaptive Color, and Smart Charging disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Tested with Windows 11 Version 10.0.22621 (22H2). Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.

      Surface Laptop Studio 2 with Intel® Core™ i7-13700H Processor, NVIDIA® GeForce™ RTX 4060 Laptop GPU, 2TB, 64GB RAM. Testing conducted by Microsoft in July-August 2023 using preproduction Windows 11 software and preproduction Surface Laptop Studio 2 Intel® Core™ i7-13800H Processor, NVIDIA® GeForce™ RTX 4060 Laptop GPU, 2TB, 64GB RAM devices. Testing consisted of full battery discharge with a mixture of active use and modern standby. The active use portion consists of (1) a web browsing test accessing eight popular websites over multiple open tabs, (2) a productivity test utilizing Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel, OneNote and Outlook, and (3) a portion of time with the device in use with idle applications. All settings were default except screen brightness was set to 150 nits with Auto-Brightness, Adaptive Color, and Smart Charging disabled. Wi-Fi was connected to a network. Tested with Windows 11 Version 10.0.22621 (22H2). Battery life varies significantly with settings, usage, and other factors.
    • [3] Surface Pro display has rounded corners within a standard rectangle. When measured as a standard rectangular shape the screen is 12” diagonally (actual viewable area is less).
    • [4] 6GHz band not available in all regions.
    • [5] Surface Pro 13" with 5G only. 5G not available in all areas; compatibility and performance depends on carrier network, plan and other factors. See carrier for details and pricing.
    • [6] Might vary depending on manufacturing processes.
    • [7] 32GB, 64GB memory and 1TB storage options available for Surface Pro 13" with Wi-Fi only.
    • [8] Requires Dolby Vision® encoded content and video.
    • [9] This device does not contain user serviceable parts. Hard drive is only removable by an authorized technician following Microsoft provided instructions.
    • [10] In select markets, only specific configurations come with 39W Surface power supply. Fast Charging (from 5% to 80%) is supported with minimum 45W Surface power supply or USB-C® PD charger, sold separately. Testing was conducted at Microsoft in February 2024, using pre-production devices, software, and minimum of 45W USB-C® charger under controlled conditions. The device was powered on to the desktop screen with default display brightness settings. Actual charge time will vary based on operating conditions. Measured at typical office ambient temperature of 23C.
    • [11] 13.8” Surface Laptop comes with 39W Surface power supply. Fast charging (from 5% to 80%) is supported with minimum 65W Surface power supply or USB-C® PD charger, sold separately. Testing was conducted at Microsoft in February 2024, using pre-production devices, software, and minimum of 65W USB-C® charger under controlled conditions. The device was powered on to the desktop screen with default display brightness settings. Actual charge time will vary based on operating conditions. Measured at typical office ambient temperature of 23C.
    • [12] Subscription required (sold separately).
    • [13] Activation required.
    • [14] Activation required for Xbox Game Pass Ultimate. Xbox Game Pass ultimate one month trial accessible through Xbox Play. You can use it to play games on Xbox One and Xbox Series Consoles, and stream select games where available through Xbox Play on supported devices.
    • [15] Colors available on selected models only. Available colors, sizes, finishes, and processors may vary by store, market, and configuration.
    • [16] Microsoft’s Limited Warranty is in addition to your consumer law rights.
    • [17] Weight not including Type Cover.
    • [18] For covered defects and/or accidental damage, maximum of two (2) claims. Each claim is subject to a $49 deductible.
    • [19] Voice focus requires activation, requires Windows 11 and is available in apps which use integrated device microphones and use certain Windows audio processing modes.
    • [20] Requires Dolby Atmos® encoded content and audio.
    • [21] eSIM support may vary by carrier.
    • [22] Applies to Surface Pro Keyboard with pen storage, Surface Pro Flex Keyboard, and Surface Pro Signature Keyboard.
    • [23] Surface Laptop Studio 2 display has rounded corners within a standard rectangle. When measured as a standard rectangular shape the screen is 14.36” diagonally (actual viewable area is less).
    • [24] Compatible camera required; available effects vary by device. See aka.ms/copilotpluspcs.
    • [25] Voice Clarity requires Windows 11 and is available in apps which use integrated device microphones and use certain Windows audio processing modes.
    • [26] Activation required for Xbox Game Pass Ultimate. Xbox Game Pass ultimate one month trial accessible through Xbox Play. You can use it to play games on Xbox One and Xbox Series Consoles, and stream select games where available to Surface Laptop Studio 2 through Xbox Play and other supported devices. Not currently available in China.
    • [29] In select markets, only specific configurations come with Surface 45W USB-C® Wall Charger. Fast charging is supported with USB Type-C PD charger rated at minimum charging to 60W or higher (sold separately). Testing conducted by Microsoft in March 2025. For details on fast charging see - USB-C® and Fast Charging for Surface - Microsoft Support.
    • [30] Removable flash storage is only available on Surface Laptop 1TB storage configuration. Flash storage Retention is only available on Microsoft Surface devices in which the flash storage is marketed as removable per the Technical Specifications. Flash storage Retention is included in both Extended Hardware Service Plus and Microsoft Complete for Business Plus and is also available as an Optional Add-on when purchasing Microsoft Extended Hardware Service and Microsoft Complete for Business. Devices returned to Microsoft with a missing flash storages are subject to a flash storage replacement fee unless the device is enrolled in the flash storage Retention offer.
    • [31] Features differ depending on the exact model you choose.
    • [32] Windows Autopilot requires qualifying volume license or subscription.
    • [33] Supported via Device Firmware Configure Interface (DFCI) through Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Microsoft Intune). See Manage DFCI on Surface devices.
    • [34] Built in on all Copilot+ PCs and hardware dependent on other Windows 11 PCs.
    • [35] Hardware-dependent.
    • [36] Facial and fingerprint recognition work with devices equipped with the correct sensors. PIN, facial, and fingerprint recognition require Windows Hello to be set up.
    • [37] Availability may vary by device and region.
